lesson.menu_title: Should I or Shouldn't I? -- Modals for Advice
lesson.menu_desc: Dar e receber conselho antes da viagem da delegacao para Seul: should e shouldn't para conselho, could para sugestao, might para incerteza -- Bruno pede a orientacao da Maria Clara. Vocabulario: recommend, suggest, consider, advise, avoid, risk, option, prepare -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 11 -- Should I or Shouldn't I?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 11 -- Should I or Shouldn't I?

listenings.text: Hi Maria Clara, it is Bruno. A quick question before the Seoul trip. I need your advice. First, the documents. I have my passport, but I might not have the visa in time. Should I travel anyway, or should I wait? Second, the meeting. In my opinion, we could invite the rector to the opening session, but I am not sure. What do you recommend? Third, the interpreter. She might cancel again, and I do not want to take a big risk. You always say, better to be safe than sorry, so should I prepare a second plan? Please call me back with your advice. Thank you.

Good afternoon. This is a short message from the institutional travel office, with some advice for the delegation to Seoul. Please listen before you travel. First, your documents. You should carry your passport and your visa together, and you shouldn't put them in your luggage. Keep them with you. Second, the money. We recommend you take a little cash in local currency, because you might need it at the airport. Third, the schedule. You should confirm every appointment one day before, and you shouldn't change the times without a good reason. And one more thing. You might feel tired after the long flight, so we advise you to rest before the first meeting. It is always better to be safe than sorry. Have a good trip.

items.situation: Bruno asks: "My visa might not be ready. Should I travel anyway?" Give him advice.
tips: No, you shouldn't travel without your visa.
You should call the embassy first, and you could wait one more day.

items.situation: Bruno asks: "Should I invite the rector to the opening session?" Give him advice.
tips: Yes, I recommend you invite him.
You should invite the rector, but you might want to confirm the date first.

items.situation: Bruno asks: "The interpreter might cancel. What should I do?" Give him advice.
tips: You should prepare a second option.
Better to be safe than sorry -- I advise you to have a second plan.

items.situation: Bruno asks: "Should I put my documents in my luggage?" Give him advice.
tips: No, you shouldn't put them in your luggage.
You should keep your passport and your visa with you.

items.situation: Bruno asks: "When should I confirm the appointments?" Give him advice.
tips: You should confirm every appointment one day before.
You shouldn't change the times without a good reason.

items.situation: Bruno asks: "Are you ready for Seoul?" Answer, and use the expression from today.
tips: In my opinion, we are ready -- but better to be safe than sorry.
I speak English, and I am improving, so I am ready for Seoul.
